title: Sivaganga Palace
tagline: Sivaganga Palace — heritage city of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Sivaganga Palace is a palace in Sivaganga district, Tamil Nadu, southern India, about 40 kilometres (25 mi) from Madurai.
overview.description: Sivaganga Palace is a palace in Sivaganga district, Tamil Nadu, southern India, about 40 kilometres (25 mi) from Madurai. It is an old royal palace, with many historical connections. The palace was used as residence by queens Velu Nachiyar (1780–90), Vellacci Nachiyar (1790–93) and Rani Kaathama Nachiar (1864–77). No remains of the original Sivaganga Palace exist, but a new palace, known as "Gowri Vilasam", was built by Padamathur Gowry Vallabha Thevar (1801-1829) in the year 19th century.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Sivagangai
topPlaces.description: The Diocese of Sivagangai is located at the southeast corner of Bay of Bengal, 45 km east of Madurai city, in Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Sivaganga
topPlaces.description: Sivaganga (Tamil: [siʋaɡəŋɡaɪ]) is a town and headquarters of the Sivaganga district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is known for the 16th-century Sivagangai Fort, located in Town Centre.

topPlaces.name: Madagupatti
topPlaces.description: Madagupatti is a village in Sivaganga district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This village is part of Sivaganga taluk in the Sivaganga district.

topPlaces.name: Nattarasankottai
topPlaces.description: Nattarasankottai is a village in Sivaganga district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Senthi Udayanathapuram
topPlaces.description: Senthi Udayanathapuram (also spelled senthiudayanathapuram) is a village in the Sivaganga district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The village is known for its graphite mineral resources and graphite mines operated by Tamil Nadu Minerals (TAMIN), a state-owned by mining company.

items.note: The Diocese of Sivagangai is located at the southeast corner of Bay of Bengal, 45 km east of Madurai city, in Tamil Nadu, India.
Sivaganga (Tamil: [siʋaɡəŋɡaɪ]) is a town and headquarters of the Sivaganga district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is known for the 16th-century Sivagangai Fort, located in Town Centre.
Madagupatti is a village in Sivaganga district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This village is part of Sivaganga taluk in the Sivaganga district.
Nattarasankottai is a village in Sivaganga district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

Senthi Udayanathapuram (also spelled senthiudayanathapuram) is a village in the Sivaganga district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The village is known for its graphite mineral resources and graphite mines operated by Tamil Nadu Minerals (TAMIN), a state-owned by mining company.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sivaganga Palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sivaganga Palace is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sivaganga Palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (43 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (41 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sivaganga Palace?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sivaganga Palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sivaganga Palace?
faq.a: Stays in Sivaganga Palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sivaganga Palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are Diocese of Sivagangai, Sivaganga, Madagupatti, Nattarasankottai, Senthi Udayanathapuram.
